title: Low emission zone modelling for cities
summary: A demonstrator using Copernicus land cover, traffic proxies, and CAMS air quality data to help cities design and evaluate low emission zone boundaries.
narrative:
## What it does
This demonstrator lets a city planner draw candidate low emission zone (LEZ) boundaries and see modelled air quality impact, drawing on CAMS regional air quality fields, CLMS land cover/urban atlas data for context, and Sentinel-5P NO2 observations to validate model output against what's actually been observed.

## Why it matters
Many European cities are expanding or introducing LEZs under national clean-air legislation, but boundary design is often based on traffic counts alone. Adding a satellite-validated air quality layer helps planners target zones where the modelled benefit is real and observable, and gives a defensible evidence base for public consultation.

## Status
This is a placeholder entry describing a demonstrator concept; no live instance is published yet.
